findings: DOABILITY: Step 10 (image_fill_area to "place the white-bg hero onto a solid pure-#FFFFFF square 1:1 canvas... extends the white field to a square") is not connector-doable: image_fill_area is a SOLID-color fill of a selection WITHIN the existing canvas — it cannot extend/grow the canvas or composite an image onto a larger square canvas. Squaring a non-square image requires canvas padding/extension, which only the banned image_generative_expand does. The reverify explicitly swapped the banned generative_expand for image_fill_area, but image_fill_area physically cannot produce a square 2048x2048 canvas from the non-square source. All other 15 steps are real, doable [C] Adobe actions.  |  PRES

DOABILITY: Step 16 (banner) uses image_fill_area to "place the graded ~2400x2400 square master on a solid white 16:9 2560x1440 canvas, bracelet offset, with text-safe negative space." image_fill_area is solid-color fill of a selection WITHIN an existing image — explicitly "NOT a compositor" — and cannot enlarge/re-shape a square into a wider-than-source canvas with the subject offset and added white space. That is canvas extension = image_generative_expand (banned) or local compositing. The reverify note itself admits step 16 was an image_generative_expand outpaint "replaced by image_fill_area" — a mislabel, not a real fix. Its output spec (2560x1440, wider than the 2400 source) is unachieva

IP/NAMING: The full_brief and one_line_ask explicitly name three real, living, world-famous footballers — Lionel Messi, Cristiano Ronaldo, and Kylian Mbappé — as the depicted subjects of a commercial poster. These are real people with right-of-publicity/likeness protection, so the brief as delivered commissions grading a poster built around named celebrity likenesses. The grounding_note and input gen_prompts try to launder this ("faces generic, not actual celebrities", no real flags/logos, fictional Continental Cup), and the actual generated assets are generic/unbranded — but the brief text the agent receives still names the three real players. This is the same category as using a real perso

IP/NAMING: The client is NOT fictional. The spec's own grounding_note states the brand is 'VERBATIM' from a real Upwork brief ('cand_52'): 'John Paul the Great Academy, a Catholic classical school in Lafayette, Louisiana' — a specific, named, geographically-pinned real religious institution whose name also invokes a real historical/religious figure (Pope John Paul II, 'John Paul the Great'). Real 'John Paul the Great' Catholic schools/academies exist. The benchmark requires the CLIENT to be an invented/fictional brand; using a verbatim real school (name + denomination + city + founding year 2006 + anniversary) violates that rule and carries real-org/trademark and sensitivity risk. Delivery-p

DOABILITY: Step 8 (image_fill_area) cannot do what it claims: it fills a masked region of an EXISTING image with solid color within the current canvas — it cannot enlarge/extend a 1024x1024 frame onto a new 1080x1920 canvas or composite the frame onto a larger solid ground. Going square→vertical canvas is a canvas-extension op the connector only offers via image_generative_expand (BANNED). No non-generative solid-fill path achieves this. Worse, the spec still carries live references to the banned capability the reverify claims was removed: output spec line 72 says the cover is "outpainted top+bottom to 9:16"; chaining_note (line 270) says "→ generative_expand to 9:16 (8)"; difficulty_rationa

IP/NAMING: "Beacon Mutual Insurance Group" is confusingly close to a REAL company: Beacon Mutual Insurance Company is an actual, well-known Rhode Island insurance carrier (workers' comp). The core mark "Beacon Mutual Insurance" is nearly identical and in the exact same industry (insurance); adding "Group" and switching the line to personal auto does not clear the collision. Should be renamed to a clearly invented, non-colliding brand.  |  TOP: Client brand "Beacon Mutual Insurance Group" collides with the real Beacon Mutual Insurance Company (a RI insurance carrier) — same industry, near-identical name; must be renamed to a clearly fictional brand.

DOABILITY: Step 10 uses asset_preview_file to pull "only the flagged pages" of the proof PDF into a new subset flagged-pages PDF. Preview tools generate previews of a file; extracting/subsetting specific pages into a new multi-page PDF is a page-selection/split operation the preview tool does not natively perform, so the tool mapping is imperfect. The operation itself is benign and non-generative (no annotations drawn, no new content), so this is a mapping stretch, not a banned capability. All other steps (create folders, upload init/finalize, document_merge_data_layout [T] on client-authored .indd, document_render_layout, document_convert_pdf, asset_preview_file/inline_preview inspect, medi
